text:
Page 1 of 6 POLICY GUIDELINES FOR APPOINTMENT OF PROFESSOR OF PRACTICE BY THE HIGHER EDUCATION INSTITUTIONS (HEIs) IN PAKISTAN Higher Education Commission Islamabad 
2025 Page 2 of 6 Policy Guidelines for Appointment of Professor of Practice by the Higher Education Institutions 
(HEIs) in Pakistan 
 
1. Background: The rapidly evolving academic landscape across the globe makes it crucial for the HEIs to 
integrate practical application with theoretical knowledge in its study programs, which in turn 
supports bridging the gap between the academia and industry/profession.

Universities across the 
world have adopted approaches to provide students with progressive professional instructions from 
faculty having extensive practical experience in the profession. One such strategy being practiced is 
including Professors of Practice in the faculty of professional degree programs who bring substantial 
professional experience and industry/sectoral insights to the academia. Having such professionals 
within the faculty also brings distinction to the academic discipline and improvement in the quality 
and repute of the universities.

Pakistan is 6th populous country of the world and as per UNDP statistics, 64 % of its 
population is below 30 years old and 29% fall between the age of 15-29 years. It could become a 
potential resource for the uplift of economy if skill-based education is provided at the universities. The curriculum of the academic programs demands continuous update for making it relevant and 
aligned with the requirements of industry/profession.

At the same time, the students at HEIs need 
an enabling environment where they can receive leadership and mentorship support for developing 
innovative and entrepreneurial skills and capabilities along with professional knowledge. Engaging 
experts from various fields as faculty can enable the HEIs to address these challenges and utilize 
their expertise for achieving excellence in professional qualifications of the students.

While the 
significance of value gained by universities from having such professionals can vary, the fundamental 
principles/guidelines that support appointment of Professors of Practice within the universities/HEIs 
include the following:  
 
i. Expertise in industry/profession: The professionals with significant practical experience in 
their respective fields including but not limited to industry, entrepreneurship, government, 
journalism, law, clinical or creative/performing arts etc. can help students to gain knowledge 
related to the profession.

It will help students to learn and demonstrate practical skills which 
are in demand in the market.  
 
ii. Acting as Bridge Between Academia and Industry/Profession: They will serve as a bridge 
between the Academia and the Industry/Profession. HEIs are expected to develop 
partnerships, collaboration and knowledge transfer among various entities. Such 
collaboration can lead to collaborative research projects, internships, and job placements 
for students, and will also foster the culture of innovation and entrepreneurship. Page 3 of 6 
 
iii.

Improving Curriculum: The practical experience of professionals can contribute to 
curriculum development as their first-hand knowledge in the domain will make it more 
practical. They are expected to incorporate industrial/sectoral/professional trends and 
practices into the academic programs. It will help students to gain skills which are relevant 
to the industry and/or profession for better job opportunities and better service-oriented 
market. 
  
iv.

Professional Development: Professors of Practice can also mentor students, providing them 
with guidance, mentorship, and networking opportunities. This mentorship can help 
students navigate their career paths, make informed decisions, and develop the soft skills 
necessary for success in the workplace. 
 
v. Enhanced Employability: Engagement of experienced professionals will help the Universities 
to offer qualifications with skills and practical experience.

It will help the graduates to get 
employment on immediate basis because graduates with strong theoretical and relevant 
professional knowledge are always preferred by various entities. Objectives: 
• Inducting individuals with extensive practical experience to enrich the educational 
experience for students by providing real-world insights and knowledge. 
• Bringing in practitioners who can offer unique perspectives and skills that complement 
traditional academic teaching, thereby enhancing the quality and relevance of 
educational programs. 
•

Encouraging innovative teaching methods and approaches that bridge the gap between 
theory and practice, preparing students for success in their future careers. 
• Contributing to scholarly activities, such as applied research and industry collaborations, 
to advance knowledge and address real-world challenges in relevant fields. 
• Attracting renowned practitioners who can enhance the reputation of the institution 
and create networking opportunities for students, faculty, and alumni within relevant 
industries and professions. 
•

Offering mentorship and guidance to students based on practical experience, helping 
them navigate career paths and make informed decisions about their professional goals. Page 4 of 6 
 
2. Eligibility Criteria for Appointment/Engagement Professionals who are distinguished practitioners, demonstrate eminence in their areas of 
expertise and have sustained accomplishments can be hired as Professor of Practice within the HEIs. The following criteria shall be considered for their selection: 
 
i.

Professional Experience: Minimum of 15 years of professional experience in a relevant 
industry/field. In case a person holds a Masters/PhD degree in Practice (from HEC recognized university/HEI), 
that will be credited/counted as professional experience (maximum 5 years for PhD including M.Phil. 
and 2 years for M.Phil./MS). Professional experience means practical experience of the industry/sector/area, however, an 
expert having both the practical experience and some teaching/coaching experience at an academic 
set up can be also considered.

Candidates are required to have extensive practical/professional experience and achievements 
in their respective fields. This could include significant work experience in industry, government, 
non-profit organizations, or other relevant sectors. They should have in-depth knowledge and practical skills relevant to the courses they will be 
teaching. They must hold an applicable license/ relevant certification, where necessary. 
 
ii. Educational Background: A traditional academic background (such as a Ph.D.) is preferable 
but not mandatory.

Candidates should possess at least a bachelor’s degree/Level-6 
qualification in their field. In some cases (as determined through need assessment), a 
terminal degree or equivalent professional qualification may be preferred.  
iii. Reputation and Recognition: A strong reputation within the relevant profession or 
academic community can be a significant asset. This might be demonstrated through 
awards, honors, collaborative projects, leadership roles, or keynote speeches at 
conferences and events.  
iv. Commitment 
to Education:

Candidates 
should 
demonstrate 
a 
passion 
for 
teaching/education and a willingness to engage with students, provide mentorship and 
contribute to the academic community. Page 5 of 6 
 
3. Roles and Responsibilities: 
i.

Teaching 
• Conduct lectures in accordance with institutional guidelines with particular 
emphasis on quality and improving student experience. 
• Introduce fresh courses, develop and design educational programs, curriculum and 
course materials. 
• Collaborate with resident faculty members to organize workshops, seminars, 
special lectures, and training sessions. 
• Contribute to development of initiatives relevant to the research, education or 
enterprise mission of a faculty. 
 
ii.

Entrepreneurial /Industry Guidance 
• Motivate and mentor students to engage in innovation and entrepreneurial 
endeavors 
• Emphasize stronger partnerships between academia/industry/sector. Undertake 
collaborative projects or provide consultancy services alongside regular faculty 
members. The concerned HEI may assign any other role/task to the Professor of Practice, through the 
approval of the Vice Chancellor/Head of the Institute, that contributes towards achievement of the 
objective anticipated from the role. 
 
4.

Procedure for Selection: 
• The Higher Education Institution (HEI) may invite applications from the eminent 
serving/retired experts for the position of Professor of Practice through an advertisement or 
head hunt who qualify the laid down criteria.  
• The Selection Board, after interviewing the eligible candidates, may submit its 
recommendations to the Syndicate or Statutory Body of the HEI for his/her engagement. 
• The HEIs can appoint Professors of Practice up to 10 % of the sanctioned posts. 
 
5. Terms of Appointment /Engagement 
i.

Contract Duration: The appointment will be purely on a contract basis (Full-time/Part-
time/Adjunct). The duration of the appointment/engagement may span up to one year 
initially. Upon completion of the initial term or any subsequent extension, the Higher Education Institution (HEI) may evaluate and determine the possibility of further 
extension. The HEI may establish its own evaluation process for extensions, considering 
the contributions and needs of the experts serving as Professors of Practice.

The 
maximum period of service for a Professor of Practice within a single institution may be 
for a period of three years with the spirit that existing faculty will get advantage of Page 6 of 6 
 
interaction with industry/professional experts and then carry forward the gained 
knowledge in teaching and research. 
ii. Workload: The university concerned shall define the workload including teaching hours, 
mentoring sessions, and other commitments as per the requirement of the program.  
iii. Compensation/Salary Package: The salary package for the appointments of Professor of

Practice shall be the same as that of the tenure track appointment of the same rank. Alternatively, a competitive remuneration package, aligned with industry/sector 
standards and HEI’s policies, must be defined and mutually agreed between the HEI and 
the selected expert. In any of the above options, the concerned HEI will pay the remuneration out of its own 
resources and there will be no financial liability on HEC.  
iv. Performance Evaluation: Host institutes will evaluate performance of Professor of

Practice on the basis of teaching effectiveness, student feedback, contributions to 
university’s objectives and/or any other indicator (s) HEI seems relevant to assess 
effectiveness of the position. 
v. Dismissal/Other Conditions: Dismissal of a Professor of Practice holding a fixed-term 
appointment may only occur upon maturity of the contract, however, dismissal shall be 
executed prematurely on the grounds of professional incompetence, serious personal or 
professional misconduct. A notice period of 30 days will be allowed by the HEIs in cases 
of dismissal or resignation.

The Professor of Practice appointments may not be converted to tenure-track positions 
and they are also not eligible for academic administrative appointments at and above the 
department chair level. The appointed Professor of Practice is expected to follow HEI’s policies and the HEIs are 
required to address all the appointment matters/terms and conditions in the contract. Under these policy guidelines, universities/HEIs may appoint only a ‘Professor of Practice,’ 
with no provision for the appointment of Assistant or Associate Professors of Practice.

Page 1 of 13 FRAMEWORK FOR HEC DISTINGUISHED NATIONAL PROFESSOR (DNP) PROGRAM Higher Education Commission Islamabad 
2025 Page 2 of 13 FRAMEWORK FOR HEC DISTINGUISHED NATIONAL PROFESSOR (DNP) 
1. Purposes and Principles For the purpose of recognizing the achievements of outstanding academicians and researchers, the 
title of Distinguished National Professor (DNP) shall be awarded to scholars who are leaders in their 
respective fields and have made significant contributions to their academic disciplines at national 
and international levels.

DNP is a prestigious title and an exceptional honor, which shall be awarded solely based on merit as 
assessed against prescribed eligibility and evaluation criteria as well as upon the recommendations 
of a high-level HEC selection committee, mentioned at Section-4.    
2. Eligibility and Evaluation Criteria The title of DNP shall only be awarded to full-time permanent serving Professors with at least 05 
years of experience as Professor (BPS/Tenured/Long-term contract) at an HEC recognized public or 
private sector university/HEI in Pakistan.

The Distinguished National Professors will retain the title for life however, the associated financial 
benefits will cease 3 years after selection or on the date of their superannuation, whichever is 
earlier.  
2.1 Evaluation Criteria. The purpose is to establish that the nominees are professionals of 
international eminence who are active in their respective fields and would be able to make 
significant contributions to the educational vision of the university.

Candidates shall be 
evaluated based on their excellence in teaching, research, academic leadership, and 
contributions at national and international levels. Emphasis would be placed on quality and 
impact, rather than quantity. The following indicators & weights will be used in this regard:

2.2 Guidelines for grading the candidates The following principles will be observed while grading the candidates under various indicators, 
given at Table-1 above: 
i.

Research Contributions:  
• The maximum marks to be given under “Invention/Innovation” should be 10, even if the 
candidate obtains full marks in all indicators under this head.  
• The candidates will be ranked within one broad subject category on percentile basis, 
which means that a candidate with the highest Cumulative Impact Factor of his/her 
publications as a Corresponding Author in a given broad category of subjects will get 
maximum marks (100%).

Other candidates will get marks accordingly (e.g. if 05 is taken 
as the benchmark for the highest Cumulative IF, then the others will get 04 marks for IF 
of 04, 03 marks for IF of 3 and so on).  
• Similarly, the candidate with the highest number of total citations, h-index and citation 
per article will get maximum marks (100%), while others will get marks accordingly.  
• In case, there is only one candidate in the broad discipline, top percentile scores to be 
used will be mean/average of top scores candidates in all disciplines. 
ii.

Mentorship 
• The highest Cumulative Impact Factor of publications emerged from PhD thesis of 
respective PhD graduate(s) will get maximum marks (100%), while others will get marks 
accordingly. 
iii. Research Grants: 
• Marks on research projects will be given only, if the applicant is Principal Investigator 
(PI). 
iv. Recognition & Engagement 
• Only those International Awards that are listed in the Annex-II of this framework will be 
considered for grading. Page 7 of 13 
 
• No marks will be awarded for certificate of appreciation/participation or research 
fellowship/scholarship.  
•

No marks will be assigned in case of membership of Boards, Committees & other Governing bodies within the universities. 
3. Procedure for nomination and recommendations The following procedure will be adopted for receiving nominations and finalization of 
recommendations: I. The nomination and selection of Distinguished National Professors will take place annually. II. The universities will issue a call for nomination from all faculties and departments upon 
initiation of the annual cycle by HEC. III. The application/nomination will be supported with the following: 
a.

A covering note and complete data on DNP Evaluation Parameters (Table 1).  
b. Latest CV of the candidate. 
c. Evidence/supporting documents required for achievements claimed under: 
i. Research Contributions: 
• Official 
documents 
or 
evidence 
of 
patents/service/process/crops 
(commercialized/not commercialized). 
•

List of articles published including authors, title, name of journal, year of publication, 
pages and DOI assigned along with impact factor of each article in the respective 
year of publication, as well as citation index (total citations, H-Index and citations 
per article), using Web of Science (WOS) as the data source. The weblink allowing 
access to Web of Science Profile ID must also be provided.  
ii. Mentorship: 
•

Evidence/notifications of supervision as main supervisor, list of PhD scholars 
supervised along with research titles of PhD thesis and list of publications emerged 
from PhD thesis combined with IF of respective journal(s), degree completion 
year/status, copies of degrees and registration number in HEC Directory. 
• A statement by candidate that publications listed under this category are from PhD 
thesis under his/her supervision (as main supervisor) and that he/she is 
corresponding author in all publication under this category.

Incorrect statement can 
lead to debar for three years, applying to DNP; debarring to other facilities of HEC, 
such as research funding and other awards may also be affected depending on level 
of violation of ethics. Page 8 of 13 
 
iii. Research Grants: 
• List of local/international research grants won/received including information on 
funding source, start & end dates of project, grant amount and role of candidate i.e. PI/Co-PI. 
iv. Services to Community: 
•

Evidence/official documents of campaigns, policies (developed or contributed in its 
formulation), tv/media shows, articles in print media etc. 
v. Recognition, Engagement and other Contributions:  
• List and certificates of national/international awards received. 
• Notifications/evidence of editorship of journal including weblink of the respective 
journal as well as evidence for Chair of Academic Chair(s).   
• List and letters of fellowships/memberships along with engagement status 
(Lifetime/specific term). 
•

Letters/evidence of industry collaboration (s) and data/reports on outcomes.  
• List and dates of reputed conferences/seminars (regularly held at least for the 
last 05 years), where the candidate was invited as keynote speaker.  
vi. Endorsement: 
• List of three referees with names, academic title, postal address, email IDs 
(institutional email, in case still serving) as well as their short biography and 
assessment of DNP candidate’s contributions/impact in the relevant field(s).

At 
least one of the referees must describe/assess the impact of the DNP candidate’s 
contribution in service to the university, community and country. 
• A statement on Conflict of Interest, that the candidate has not collaborated with 
the referee at least in last 5 years, either in terms of joint publication, co-
supervision of a graduate student, or Co-PI in a research grant. 
• It will be the responsibility of the university to examine profiles of the referees 
who are themselves qualified and willing to make judgement of the DNP 
candidates.  
d.

Two one-page statements prepared by the candidates, covering the below:  
i. Contributions in relevant field at national and international level and its impact.  
ii. Services rendered at the community/university level and its impact. IV. Each participating university will set up a permanent/Standing selection committee, to be 
chaired by the Vice Chancellor including the Registrar, Director QEC, up to two existing DNPs 
and up to three senior Deans to review the applications and recommend one candidate for 
the award of DNP. Page 9 of 13 V.

The selection committee would make the decision and convey the results to HEC within 
deadlines specified by HEC. VI. All the documents of the recommended nominee would be provided by the universities to HEC in the form of a dossier, duly tagged and page numbers inserted. Soft copies of the 
dossier must also be provided in a USB or uploaded on online storage/file sharing platforms. VII. Candidates recommended by the universities shall be evaluated at national level by the HEC Selection Committee (Table 2), which shall meet annually to finalize its recommendations. VIII.

The candidates can be called to appear in an interview if the national level selection 
committee deems it necessary. IX. Only those whose applications and supporting documents were submitted on or before the 
specified deadline and recommended by the parent university will be considered for the 
award of title. X. The National Level Selection Committee will review the recommendations and approve 
selection of the DNPs under the below subjects/categories. A maximum of five DNPs will be 
selected per cycle with not more than one DNP in any of the below mentioned 
subjects/categories:  
i.

Education.  
ii. Social Sciences, Journalism and Information 
iii. Information and Communication Technology 
iv. Health and Welfare 
v. Arts and Humanities 
vi. Natural Sciences, Mathematics and Statistics.   
vii. Engineering, Manufacturing and Construction.  
viii. Agriculture, Forestry, Fisheries and Veterinary 
ix. Services 
x. Business, Administration and Law 
 
4. Selection Committee. The composition of the national level selection committee shall be as follows: Table 2: DNP Selection Committee HEC 
1. Executive Director, HEC Chairperson 
2. Three (03) existing DNPs from different fields

Members 
3. Chairperson, Vice Chancellors Committee Member 
4. Director General/In charge (Academics), HEC Member  
5. Director (Academics), HEC Secretary The Committee may co-opt any member in its constitution, if required 
 
(Refer to “Terms and Conditions” at Annex-I for further details). Page 10 of 13 Annex-I TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE HEC DNP AWARD 
1. Eligibility 
a. Full-time permanent serving Professors (BPS/Tenured/Long-term contract) with at least 05 
years of experience as Professor at an HEC recognized public or private sector university/ HEI 
of Pakistan. 
i.

The applicants must be under 60 years of age. 
ii. Individuals who have disciplinary proceedings pending against them or have been 
penalized on charges of plagiarism or other forms of misconduct are not eligible. 
iii. There should be no adverse remarks in their ACRs during the previous five (05) years. 
b. A Professor who had received the HEC DNP award under the old framework can apply 
provided he/she is eligible. Also, if she/he reapplies, will have to provide his/her information 
and supporting documents for the entire intervening period since the receipt of the award.

The university’s internal committee would have the right to examine the entire dossier in 
order to judge eligibility for the subsequent award. 
c. Candidates for DNP award will not be part of the University’s Selection Committee. Similarly, Conflict of Interest if any, has to be declared by the selection committee members that the 
candidate has not collaborated with any member, at least in the last 5 years, either in terms 
of joint publication, co-supervision of a graduate student, or Co-PI in a Research Grant. 
 
2. Terms 
a.

The Distinguished National Professor will retain the title for life however, the associated 
financial benefits will cease 3 years after selection or on the date of his/her superannuation, 
whichever is earlier. 
b. The Distinguished Professor awardee will receive an honorarium of Rs. 200,000/- on bi-
annual basis for 3 years or until superannuation date, whichever is earlier. Funds for the DNP 
remunerations will be provided by HEC to the universities. 
c.

It shall be the responsibility of the host university to monitor the performance of the DNP, 
which may be shared with HEC on annual basis.   
d. The DNP title may not be terminated from the holder except in case of proven charges of 
gross misconduct or plagiarism, which will require a process to follow. 
 
3. Selection Procedure 
a. All names will be submitted by the universities to HEC after a transparent scrutiny process.  
b. The initial screening will take place at the university level.

The universities are required to 
ensure circulation of the opportunity across all the departments, within the application 
period, to ensure fair competition and maximum participation.  
i. The university will notify a Selection Committee for this purpose (see Section 3 
“Procedure for nomination and finalization”).  
ii. The Selection Committee will undertake the university-level scrutiny, and submit its 
recommendation, duly signed by all members, to HEC 
iii.

While submitting the nomination, the Selection Committee at the university will certify 
that all eligibility criteria have been met. Page 11 of 13 
 
iv. The department head and dean of relevant faculty will add their remarks in the 
candidates’ dossier for facilitation of the Selection Committee. 
v. The university will set up a mechanism to address grievances if any. 
c. Nominations and supporting documents received after due date, will not be considered.  
d.

The nominees are required to provide detailed justification of the proposed title in the 
covering letter, specifically addressing the criteria outlined in Table 1. The CV must include 
supporting information, consistent with the required criteria. 
 
4. HEC will only entertain nominations received according to the prescribed channel and 
procedure. HEC's decision will be based solely on the documentary record provided by the 
university on or before the deadline, and on the recommendations of the university’s Selection Committee. 
5.

HEC has the right not to make an award in a particular year or under a particular category. 
6. Maximum of 05 Distinguished National Professors will be appointed in a cycle under the list of 
broad categories of disciplines (mentioned at Section 3 “Procedure for nomination and 
recommendations”) and that not more than one from a broad category of discipline will be 
appointed in a cycle. The decision of HEC will be final. 
7.

Anyone who provides false or misleading evidence will be barred for three years from the 
competition as well as from other facilities of HEC (Research Funding or Awards). An award 
granted on the basis of false and misleading evidence will be withdrawn and penalty 
implemented. 
  
8. Roles and activities of DNP

The DNP shall engage in such activities as expected from a full professor, however, being holder 
of the national award and an extraordinary honor, he/she will also be entrusted with national 
responsibilities, including, but not limited to, the following: 
• Research-based policy advice to the government. 
• Representation of the university in international forums, including those for fundraising or 
income generation. 
• Facilitating national and international partnerships. 
• Organizing national/international conferences. 
• Contributing to improvements or innovations in research and teaching programs and 
methodologies.  
• Engaging students in community-oriented work/projects.

Page 1 of 8 FRAMEWORK FOR APPOINTMENT OF MERITORIOUS PROFESSOR IN THE HIGHER EDUCATION INSTITUTIONS (HEIs) Higher Education Commission Islamabad 
2024 Page 2 of 8 Purpose and Objective: The purpose and objective of appointing a Meritorious Professor is to recognize and reward 
academic excellence and contributions within a specific field. This title is conferred to acknowledge 
outstanding achievements in research, teaching, and service to the academic community.

The 
appointment aims to motivate continued high performance, inspire other faculty members, and 
uphold the institution's standards of excellence. Additionally, it enhances the institution's prestige 
by associating it with distinguished scholars, thus attracting top-tier students, faculty, and research 
opportunities. The universities may appoint Meritorious Professor on prescribed guidelines of the Higher Education Commission (HEC) through their statutory process. 
 
1. Provision of Posts:  
  
1.1.

The number of Professors to be promoted to BPS-22 in each university shall be 
calculated on the basis of 12.5% of the filled posts of the Professors in BPS-21. 
 
 
2. Eligibility Criteria:  
 
2.1. Only those Professors who have served at least for 05 years in BPS-21 in the university. 
 
2.2. Should have minimum of 06 research publications as a corresponding author in the past 
5 years with at least 03 research publications in the past 2 years in HEC recognized Journals.  
 
2.3. Must have supervised minimum of 03 PhDs as a main supervisor in the last 5 years. 
 
2.4.

Must have secured research funding of at least Rs. 5.0 million from external sources.  
 
3. Procedure for Promotion: 
 
3.1. Requirement for advertisement of posts as provided in the relevant section of the University Act will be dispensed with. 
 
3.2. The dossier of each eligible University Professor will be considered in the Special Selection Board (permanent/regular members of the Selection Board, one 
representative of Chairman, HEC and one representative of the Chancellor) for the 
award of BPS-22.

The recommendations of the Selection Board will be placed before 
the Syndicate/ BoG/ equivalent body for endorsement before submitting to the Chancellor for final approval. Page 3 of 8 In case Syndicate/BoG/Equivalent body does not endorse recommendation of Selection Board, the case will be referred back to Selection Board along with its 
comments for reconsideration. 
 
3.3. The Registrar Office shall prepare the cases of Professors in BPS-21 for the award of

BPS-22 and present the record of each such Professor on the proforma designed for 
this purpose, and approved as a part of the statutes along with (a) Annual Confidential Report (ACRs) for the last five years in BPS-21, and (b) a resume of the Professor and 
his/her achievements in research, teaching, university services   and community 
engagement etc. 
 
3.4. The University shall calculate the total score of each eligible applicant according to the 
parameters detailed in clause-4 (Grading Procedure).

Those who secure a minimum 
score of 60% will be presented before the Selection Board for consideration. 
 
3.5. All promotions to BPS-22 shall be effective from the date of recommendations by the Syndicate/BoG/equivalent body.  
 
4. Grading Procedure (Total 100 marks): 
 
4.1. Research Output (max. 38 marks): 
 
4.1.1. Invention/Innovation (max. 10 marks)  
 
a. Product/process or service developed only (Physical and Virtual) but not commercialized 
(max. 4 marks): 
- Patents granted (01 mark each) 
- Process or Service (01 mark each)   
 
 
b.

Product/process or service commercialized (Physical and Virtual) (max. 4 marks):  
- Patents licensed (02 marks each) 
- Process or website implemented for service (02 marks each) 
 
c. Crop variety (max. 4   marks): 
- Crop variety registered (1 mark each) 
- Crop variety commercialized (2 marks each) Note: Maximum marks under 4.1.1. “Invention/Innovation” should be 10, even if the candidate obtains full 
marks in all indicators under this head. Page 4 of 8 
 
 
4.1.2. Cumulative Impact Factor (IF) of Journal Publications (max.10 marks) 
 
4.1.2.1.

All Journal Publications (max. 5 marks) 
  
              
 
-1 mark for each IF of 40.  
-5 marks for cumulative IF of 200 and above.  
 
4.1.2.2. Journal publications as a Corresponding Author (max. 5 marks) 
 
-01 mark for each IF of 20. 
-05 marks for cumulative IF of 100 and above. 
 
4.1.3. Citation Index (max. 18 marks) 
 
4.1.3.1. Total number of citations (max. 9 marks)  
 
-1 mark for each 500 citations. 
-9 marks for total citations of 4,500 and above. 
 
4.1.3.2. H-Index (max. 9 marks) 
 
-1 mark for each h-index of 4. 
-9 marks for h-index of 36 and above. 
 
4.2.

Research Funding (max. 13 marks):   
 
-1 mark each for cumulative grant funding of Rs. 1.0 million as PI. 
-13 marks for a cumulative grant funding of Rs.13.0 million and above. 
 
4.3. Mentoring of Graduate Students (max. 12 marks): 
 
4.3.1 Number of PhD students supervised (max. 5 marks)  
           
      -1 mark for each PhD student supervised.  
 
4.3.2 Quality of PhD student supervision (max. 7 marks) 
-1 mark for each cumulative impact factor of 3 of Journal publications   emerged 
from PhD thesis. 
        
 
  -7 marks for cumulative impact factor of 21 and above. 
 
4.4.

Recognitions, Engagement, 
and Other Contributions 
in 
the 
field                            
(max. 25 marks): 
4.4.1. Awards (max. 8 marks) 
 
4.4.1.1. National (max. 5 marks): - Page 5 of 8 
 
 
-2 marks per award of excellence in Teaching (HEC’s Best University Teacher Award) or Award of Excellence in Research (Civil Awards) or Life-Time Academic Achievement Award.  
-1 mark each for other awards such as PAS awards or HEC Research/Teaching 
awards including Izaz-e-Kamal and Izaz-e-Fazeelat (max. 2 marks). 
-1 mark if the University Professor holds the title of HEC Distinguished National

Professor. 
4.4.1.2. International (max. 3 marks): - 
 
- 3 marks per international award, as per list at Annex-I.  
4.4.2. Fellowship/Membership (max. marks 7): - Membership/Fellowship of renowned international/national academies as below: -   
  
d. Fellow of PAS (2 marks). 
e. Fellow of discipline-wise professional societies (1 mark for each   with 
maximum of 2 marks).  
f. Fellow (member) of TWAS (3 marks).  
 
4.4.3. Other Professional Contribution (max. 10 marks)  
 
- Served as Chair of Academic Chairs abroad (max. 2 marks). 
- Chief Editor of a journal with IF of 0.5 to 01 (1 mark).

Chief Editor of a Journal 
with IF above 1 (2 marks) (max. 2 marks). 
- Member of Editorial Board of a Journal with an Impact Factor of 0.5 to 1 (1 
mark) and for a journal with IF of more than 1 (2 marks) (max. 2 marks).  
- 
2 marks per tangible collaboration internationally or with industry/profession 
(max. 2 marks). 
- 
1 mark for invited keynote speaker in reputed national/international 
conference (regularly held at least for last five years) (max. 2 marks). 
 
4.5.

University/Community Service (12 marks): 
 
- 
1 mark for completing a tenure as Head of Dept, Director ORIC/QEC/BASR etc. 
(max. 2 marks). 
- 
1.5 marks for completing a tenure of Dean of Faculty/Director of Institute.    
(max. 3 marks). Page 6 of 8 
 
- 
2 marks per advocacy campaign, awareness on societal issues or engagement 
in national dialogue that enabled policy formulation and/or supported social 
inclusion (max. 4 marks). 
- 
1.5 marks each on policy formulation, talk shows on national media, 
articles/columns in popular press (max. 3 marks). 
 
5. Miscellaneous: 
 
5.1.

In case of numbers in fraction, then 0.50 or above will be considered as 1 (e.g. 59.5 
or above will be 60.0 whereas 59.49 or less will be 59). 
 
5.2. A Professor appointed as Vice-Chancellor in BPS-22 shall be allowed personal grade 
of BPS-22 as Professor after he/she relinquishes the charge of the office of Vice Chancellor, provided he/she has completed one tenure as Vice Chancellor and has 
been a regular Professor in a Public Sector University prior to his/her appointment 
as Vice-Chancellor. The personal grade so granted to him shall not be counted 
towards the 12.5% quota of BPS-22.

Page 1 of 4 FRAMEWORK FOR APPOINTMENT OF PROFESSOR EMERITUS IN THE HIGHER EDUCATION INSTITUTIONS (HEIs) Higher Education Commission Islamabad 
2024 Page 2 of 4 Purpose and Objective: The purpose and objective of appointing a Professor Emeritus is to recognize and honor the 
distinguished academic service and contributions of the Professor beyond superannuation. This title 
acknowledges their professional achievements, dedication, and impact on their field, while also 
encouraging their continued involvement with the institution.

By bestowing this title, the institution 
benefits from the Emeritus Professor's ongoing engagement in research, mentorship, and 
intellectual activities, thereby enhancing its reputation and fostering a rich academic environment. The universities may appoint Professor Emeritus on prescribed guidelines of the Higher Education Commission (HEC) through their statutory process. 
 
1. Provision of Titles:  
  
1.1. The appointment of Professor Emeritus is to be decided by the Syndicate/Appointing Authority of the respective university.  
 
1.2.

The number of Professors Emeritus would vary from university to university depending 
on its size and demand, but total number should not exceed 10% of the total working 
strength of the regular working Professors in a faculty. 
 
 
2. Eligibility Criteria:  
 
2.1. University Syndicate/Appointing Authority may confer status of an Emeritus

Professorship to a Professor retired or retired with the tenured status in recognition of 
his/her scholarship and service to university education who has served university or a 
constituent institute or a Centre of Excellence of the University for a minimum period 
of 10 years as Professor. However, those who hold the title of Distinguished National Professor (DNP) or Meritorious Professor qualify to be considered even if the 
experience as Professor is less than 10 years. OR 
2.2.

A Professor who has completed at least one tenure as a Vice-Chancellor in the public 
sector university shall be conferred the status of Professor Emeritus (PE) after attaining 
the age of superannuation. He/she can apply for joining as Professor Emeritus at the 
parent university or at a university other than the parent university, preferably in a 
newly developed university.

In cases of the Vice Chancellors joining as PE at newly developed universities, other 
than the parent universities, HEC may invite three preferences from newly developed 
universities and/or the interested Vice Chancellors may themselves offer their services 
as Professor Emeritus for other such universities where their services are more valued. In that case, NoC from parent university and the consent from host university would 
be required. Young universities, where senior faculty is not yet available for PE, are

Page 3 of 4 
 
encouraged to advertise positions of PE, through approval of its Syndicate/Appointing Authority. 
 
3. Procedure:  
 
3.1. All such cases be decided by the concerned university through Syndicate/Appointing Authority in light of the HEC’s guidelines.  
 
3.2. Those who are desirous to join as Professor Emeritus in the concerned university may 
submit or get nominated by the concerned Chair or Dean in writing, preferably 06 
months before the age of superannuation. 
 
4. Terms and Conditions: 
 
4.1.

Whereas the conferment of PE status will be for life time, the financial benefits will be 
given for a maximum period of 10 years or up to the age of 70 years, whichever is 
earlier. The PE is expected to contribute to the academic life of the university to a great extent in 
consonance with the status and caliber of the title and PE shall carry no formal statutory 
administrative positions in a university, where other senior faculty is/are available for such 
positions.

However, at young universities, where senior faculty is not available to take up 
administrative positions, PE may be asked to take up administrative positions. 
 
4.2. Professor Emeritus up to the age of 65 years, would be eligible to apply for HEC 
research funding, but he/she would be required to include at least one Co-PI from the 
host Institution. 
4.3. The Professor Emeritus, at least for the period during which he/she enjoys financial 
benefits from the University, will have the following responsibilities:  
4.3.1.

Mandatory presence on campus at least for three working days a week.  
4.3.2. Provide guidance or continue research with faculty and/or students.  
4.3.3. Graduate student’s supervision.  
4.3.4. Seminars and/or writing of textbooks in the field of his/her specialization.  
4.3.5. Any other assignment by the Vice Chancellor, including statutory academic 
administrative positions temporarily, particularly in young universities where 
indigenous senior faculty is not yet available. 
 
5. Honorarium/Remuneration:  
5.1.

Professor Emeritus shall be entitled for financial benefits for a maximum period of 10 
years or up to the age of 70 years whichever is earlier and draw a consolidated amount 
of honorarium of Rs. 200,000/p.m. (all inclusive) with an annual increase of 5%. Page 4 of 4 
 
5.2. The concerned parent university will bear financial liability of Professor Emeritus out 
of its own resources for both categories of PE i.e. Professors/Vice Chancellors.

However, HEC will bear remunerations of only those category of Vice Chancellors who 
offer their services as Professor Emeritus in newly developed universities, other than 
their parent university (reference Section 2). 
  
5.3. The initial offer of appointment will be for a period of 5 years, extendable on a yearly 
basis for the purpose of financial benefits.  
 
5.4.

The conferment of Emeritus Status shall normally be for lifetime but can be terminated 
if the Professor so desired or the Syndicate terminates by ¾ majority on grounds of 
inefficiency, moral turpitude or physical or mental incapacity or gross misconduct. 
 
5.5. During the paid tenure of Professor Emeritus, he/she will not get financial benefits 
from any other university/institution/organization. In case, PE gets appointed on any 
other full time position during the paid tenure, within or outside parent university, the 
payment for PE will be discontinued for that period.

Similarly, those Vice Chancellors who join as Professor Emeritus at a newly developed 
university, other than the parent university, will also not be paid the honorarium of PE 
if he/she is employed with any other university/institution/organization, or employed 
by the host university on another position within the paid tenure of Professor Emeritus.
